mnemonics.title: ECC Key Generation Steps
mnemonics.content: KEEP PRIVATE, CALCULATE PUBLIC: Keys = Keep private number, Calculate public point via scalar multiplication

mnemonics.title: ECC Advantages
mnemonics.content: SFS - Smaller keys, Faster computations, Stronger security compared to RSA

mnemonics.title: Elliptic Curve Equation
mnemonics.content: YAX BUBBLE: y² = x³ + Ax + B (Remember the equation structure)

mnemonics.title: Point Operations
mnemonics.content: PAD - Point Addition first, then Doubling for scalar multiplication

acronyms.usage: Remember the entire cryptographic system based on elliptic curves
Recall ECC's implementation for digital signatures
Foundation of ECC security - hard to reverse scalar multiplication

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a bubble symmetric over x-axis - every point (x,y) has mirror (x,-y)
Draw line through 2 points → 3rd intersection → flip over x-axis (like ricochet)
Picture a 'lighthouse' point acting as identity element in operations
Imagine hopping point-to-point along curve like connect-the-dots game

analogies.analogy: Like a bicycle lock vs bank vault - small ECC key provides RSA-equivalent security
Like mixing paint colors - easy to combine, impossible to separate without private key
Like trying to count bounces in a pinball machine when only shown final position
